title: oxalis praetervisa
intro: Oxalis praetervisa, also known as the wandering sorrel or wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Oxalis genus. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anthraquinone compounds, which have been found to have laxative and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Oxalis praetervisa include the potential to help with constipation, skin issues, and even as a natural remedy for certain infections. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
